_comment: Win-rate acceptance band for scripts/selfplay.mjs --check. Content changes must keep the simulated win rate inside [min,max] and strictly beatable (>0) and survivable (<1). Regenerate the observed numbers with: node scripts/selfplay.mjs --runs 500

observed.note: brown r1, blue r2, green r3, red r4, Cinderwyrm (enrage apex boss) r5+, PLUS the 'Spoils of the Realms' loot affix batch (8 themed prefixes/suffixes in js/stats.js, one prefix+suffix pair per family). ~28.2% pooled over 9000 worktree-isolated batch runs (887/3000 + 1653/6000); the standalone 6000-run --check read 27.6% and PASSED the [25,75] band; deeps average ~realm 6.4 (deepest 10), deaths split ~boss>room, never 0/100%. The affix batch is deliberately BALANCE-NEUTRAL, not a power bump: a clean A/B (worktree checked out at clean origin/main, node_modules symlinked, ONLY js/stats.js changed) measured baseline 29.1% (872/3000) vs batch 29.6% (887/3000) — +0.5pt, within the ~1.2pt diff-SE, i.e. indistinguishable from zero. KEY GOTCHA for future affix work: gScore (combat.js) sums all stats equally, so armor/defense-heavy affixes are auto-equipped over equal-scored offensive gear and quietly DILUTE hero damage; since ~70% of losses are to the scaling boss, offense/HP (not raw armor) is what converts to wins. An armor-tilted first draft of this batch dropped a 500-run pulse to 24.4% (under the floor); reweighting offense/HP-first (e.g. of the Cinderwyrm [3,3,0,2,0,2,0,0,0], Venomous [0,4,0,0,1,2,0,0,0]) restored neutrality. luck (index 2) is inert in combat and gScore, left 0 in every new affix. Prior (new-boss-mechanic) baseline was 29.3% at 12000 runs; the ~1pt lower figure here is sampling noise on top of a balance-neutral change, not a regression. Regenerate with: node scripts/selfplay.mjs --runs 6000 --check
